title: Track Blood Units by ABO Type, Rh Factor, Collection Date, and Expiry
description: The system shall track each blood unit with a unique identifier, record its ABO type and Rh factor at collection, and calculate the 42-day expiry date based on the collection date.
rationale: To ensure that blood inventory is safe, traceable, and compliant with expiration regulations.
business_value: Improves patient safety by ensuring the use of fresh and appropriate blood units, reducing the risk of infections and adverse reactions.

source.description: As a blood bank manager I need to track blood units by ABO type, Rh factor, collection date, and enforce 42-day expiry, with each unit uniquely identified and its status tracked throughout its lifecycle so that I can ensure blood inventory is safe, traceable, and compliant with expiration regulations.

acceptance_criteria: A blood unit is collected with a unique ID, ABO type, Rh factor, and collection date.
The system calculates the expiry date as 42 days from collection.
A blood unit in inventory has its status changed (e.g., from available to issued).
The system updates the unit's status and logs the change.
A blood unit's expiry date has passed.
When the system checks inventory, the unit is marked as expired and is no longer available for use.
preconditions: A blood unit is collected with all necessary details.
The current date is known to calculate the expiry date.
postconditions: The system has a unique identifier for each blood unit.
ABO type and Rh factor are recorded at collection.
Expiry date is calculated and stored with the unit's details.
Status of the blood unit is tracked throughout its lifecycle.
Expired units are flagged or removed from inventory.
functional_constraints: The system must accurately calculate the expiry date based on the collection date.
The system must update the status of a blood unit and log each change.
security_requirements: Unique identifiers should be securely stored to prevent unauthorized access.
Sensitive information such as ABO type, Rh factor, and collection date should be protected.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should handle large volumes of inventory updates efficiently.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 24/7 to ensure continuous tracking of blood units.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale with increasing number of blood units and users.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should be clear and user-friendly, providing feedback on status changes.

edge_cases: What happens if the collection date is not recorded?
How should the system handle a blood unit with an invalid ABO type or Rh factor?
What if there are multiple units with the same unique identifier?
How does the system manage the inventory when there are more than 42 days between collection and expiry?
dependencies: A database to store the blood unit details, including unique ID, ABO type, Rh factor, collection date, expiry date, and status.
A user interface for managers to input and manage blood units.

interfaces.name: Blood Unit Database
interfaces.description: Stores the details of each blood unit, including unique ID, ABO type, Rh factor, collection date, expiry date, and status.

interfaces.name: Inventory Management UI
interfaces.description: Allows managers to input new units, update statuses, and view inventory details.

test_cases.description: Input a blood unit with all necessary details.
test_cases.expected_result: The system accepts the input and stores it in the database.

test_cases.description: Change the status of a blood unit from available to issued.
test_cases.expected_result: The system updates the status and logs the change.
Expired units are marked as expired and no longer available for use.
